# services.xserver

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.xserver`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.xserver.autoRepeatDelay` | `null or signed integer` | Sets the autorepeat delay (length of time in milliseconds that a key must be depressed before autorepeat starts). |
| `services.xserver.autoRepeatInterval` | `null or signed integer` | Sets the autorepeat interval (length of time in milliseconds that should elapse between autorepeat-generated keystrokes). |
| `services.xserver.autorun` | `boolean` | Whether to start the X server automatically. |
| `services.xserver.config` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | The contents of the configuration file of the X server ({file}`xorg.conf`). This option is set by multiple modules, and the configs are concatenated together. In Xorg configs the last config entries take precedence, so you may want to use `lib.mkAfter` on this option to override NixOS's defaults. |
| `services.xserver.defaultDepth` | `signed integer` | Default colour depth. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.cde.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Common Desktop Environment. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.cde.extraPackages` | `list of package` | Extra packages to be installed system wide. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.cinnamon.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the cinnamon desktop manager. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.cinnamon.extraGSettingsOverridePackages` | `list of absolute path` | List of packages for which gsettings are overridden. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.cinnamon.extraGSettingsOverrides` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Additional gsettings overrides. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.cinnamon.sessionPath` | `list of package` | Additional list of packages to be added to the session search path. Useful for GSettings-conditional autostart. Note that this should be a last resort; patching the package is preferred (see GPaste). |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.enlightenment.enable` | `boolean` | Enable the Enlightenment desktop environment. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.kodi.enable` | `boolean` | Enable the kodi multimedia center. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.kodi.package` | `package` | The kodi package to use. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.lumina.enable` | `boolean` | Enable the Lumina desktop manager |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.lxqt.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the LXQt desktop manager. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.lxqt.extraPackages` | `list of package` | Extra packages to be installed system wide. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.lxqt.iconThemePackage` | `package` | The package that provides a default icon theme. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.mate.debug` | `boolean` | Whether to enable mate-session debug messages. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.mate.enable` | `boolean` | Enable the MATE desktop environment |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.mate.enableWaylandSession` | `boolean` | Whether to enable MATE Wayland session. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.mate.extraCajaExtensions` | `list of package` | Extra extensions to add to caja. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.mate.extraPanelApplets` | `list of package` | Extra applets to add to mate-panel. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.phosh.enable` | `boolean` | Enable the Phone Shell. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.phosh.group` | `string` | The group to run the Phosh service. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.phosh.package` | `package` | The phosh package to use. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.phosh.phocConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n" or absolute path or (submodule)` | Configurations for the Phoc compositor. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.phosh.user` | `string` | The user to run the Phosh service. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.retroarch.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable RetroArch. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.retroarch.extraArgs` | `list of string` | Extra arguments to pass to RetroArch. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.retroarch.package` | `package` | The retroarch package to use. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.runXdgAutostartIfNone` | `boolean` | Whether to run XDG autostart files for sessions without a desktop manager (with only a window manager), these sessions usually don't handle XDG autostart files by default. Some services like {option}`i18n.inputMethod` and {option}`service.earlyoom` use XDG autostart files to start. If this option is not set to `true` and you are using a window manager without a desktop manager, you need to manually start them or running `dex` somewhere. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.surf-display.defaultWwwUri` | `string` | Default URI to display. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.surf-display.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable surf-display as a kiosk browser session. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.surf-display.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration options to append to `/etc/default/surf-display`. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.surf-display.hideIdlePointer` | `string` | Hide idle mouse pointer. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.surf-display.inactivityInterval` | `signed integer` | Setting for internal inactivity timer to restart surf-display if the user goes inactive/idle to get a fresh session for the next user of the kiosk. If this value is set to zero, the whole feature of restarting due to inactivity is disabled. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.surf-display.pointerButtonMap` | `string` | Disable right and middle pointer device click in browser sessions while keeping scrolling wheels' functionality intact. See pointer subcommand on `man xmodmap` for details. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.surf-display.screensaverSettings` | `strings concatenated with " "` | Screensaver settings, see `man 1 xset` for possible options. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.wallpaper.combineScreens` | `boolean` | When set to `true` the wallpaper will stretch across all screens. When set to `false` the wallpaper is duplicated to all screens. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.wallpaper.mode` | `one of "center", "fill", "max", "scale", "tile"` | The file {file}`~/.background-image` is used as a background image. This option specifies the placement of this image onto your desktop. Possible values: `center`: Center the image on the background. If it is too small, it will be surrounded by a black border. `fill`: Like `scale`, but preserves aspect ratio by zooming the image until it fits. Either a horizontal or a vertical part of the image will be cut off. `max`: Like `fill`, but scale the image to the maximum size that fits the screen with black borders on one side. `scale`: Fit the file into the background without repeating it, cutting off stuff or using borders. But the aspect ratio is not preserved either. `tile`: Tile (repeat) the image in case it is too small for the screen. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.xfce.enable` | `boolean` | Enable the Xfce desktop environment. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.xfce.enableScreensaver` | `boolean` | Enable the XFCE screensaver. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.xfce.enableWaylandSession` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the experimental Xfce Wayland session. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.xfce.enableXfwm` | `boolean` | Enable the XFWM (default) window manager. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.xfce.noDesktop` | `boolean` | Don't install XFCE desktop components (xfdesktop and panel). |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.xfce.waylandSessionCompositor` | `string` | Command line to run a Wayland compositor, defaults to `labwc --startup` if not specified. Note that `xfce4-session` will be passed to it as an argument, see `startxfce4 --help` for details. Some compositors do not have an option equivalent to labwc's `--startup` and you might have to add xfce4-session somewhere in their configurations. |
| `services.xserver.desktopManager.xterm.enable` | `boolean` | Enable a xterm terminal as a desktop manager. |
| `services.xserver.deviceSection` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Contents of the first Device section of the X server configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.digimend.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the digimend drivers for Huion/XP-Pen/etc. tablets. |
| `services.xserver.display` | `null or signed integer` | Display number for the X server. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.autoLogin.timeout` | `signed integer` | Show the greeter for this many seconds before automatic login occurs. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.background` | `absolute path or string matching the pattern ^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$` | The background image or color to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable lightdm as the display manager. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra lines to append to LightDM section. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.extraSeatDefaults` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra lines to append to SeatDefaults section. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeter.enable` | `boolean` | If set to false, run lightdm in greeterless mode. This only works if autologin is enabled and autoLogin.timeout is zero. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeter.name` | `string` | The name of a .desktop file in the directory specified in the 'package' option. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeter.package` | `package` | The LightDM greeter to login via. The package should be a directory containing a .desktop file matching the name in the 'name' option. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.enso.blur` | `boolean` | Whether or not to enable blur |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.enso.brightness` | `signed integer` | Brightness |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.enso.cursorTheme.name` | `string` | Name of the cursor theme to use for the lightdm-enso-os-greeter |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.enso.cursorTheme.package` | `package` | The capitaine-cursors package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.enso.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable enso-os-greeter as the lightdm greeter |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.enso.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration that should be put in the greeter.conf configuration file |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.enso.iconTheme.name` | `string` | Name of the icon theme to use for the lightdm-enso-os-greeter |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.enso.iconTheme.package` | `package` | The papirus-icon-theme package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.enso.theme.name` | `string` | Name of the theme to use for the lightdm-enso-os-greeter |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.enso.theme.package` | `package` | The gnome-themes-extra package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.clock-format` | `null or string` | Clock format string (as expected by strftime, e.g. "%H:%M") to use with the lightdm gtk greeter panel. If set to null the default clock format is used. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.cursorTheme.name` | `string` | Name of the cursor theme to use for the lightdm-gtk-greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.cursorTheme.package` | `package` | The adwaita-icon-theme package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.cursorTheme.size` | `signed integer` | Size of the cursor theme to use for the lightdm-gtk-greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable lightdm-gtk-greeter as the lightdm greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration that should be put in the lightdm-gtk-greeter.conf configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.iconTheme.name` | `string` | Name of the icon theme to use for the lightdm-gtk-greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.iconTheme.package` | `package` | The adwaita-icon-theme package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.indicators` | `null or (list of string)` | List of allowed indicator modules to use for the lightdm gtk greeter panel. Built-in indicators include "~a11y", "~language", "~session", "~power", "~clock", "~host", "~spacer". Unity indicators can be represented by short name (e.g. "sound", "power"), service file name, or absolute path. If set to null the default indicators are used. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.theme.name` | `string` | Name of the theme to use for the lightdm-gtk-greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.gtk.theme.package` | `package` | The gnome-themes-extra package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.lomiri.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable lomiri's greeter as the lightdm greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.mini.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable lightdm-mini-greeter as the lightdm greeter. Note that this greeter starts only the default X session. You can configure the default X session using [](#opt-services.displayManager.defaultSession). |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.mini.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration that should be put in the lightdm-mini-greeter.conf configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.mini.user` | `string` | The user to login as. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.mobile.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable lightdm-mobile-greeter as the lightdm greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.pantheon.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable elementary-greeter as the lightdm greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.cursorTheme.name` | `string` | Name of the cursor theme to use for the lightdm-slick-greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.cursorTheme.package` | `package` | The adwaita-icon-theme package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.cursorTheme.size` | `signed integer` | Size of the cursor theme to use for the lightdm-slick-greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.draw-user-backgrounds` | `boolean` | Whether to enable draw user backgrounds. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable lightdm-slick-greeter as the lightdm greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration that should be put in the lightdm-slick-greeter.conf configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.font.name` | `string` | Name of the font to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.font.package` | `package` | The ubuntu-classic package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.iconTheme.name` | `string` | Name of the icon theme to use for the lightdm-slick-greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.iconTheme.package` | `package` | The adwaita-icon-theme package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.theme.name` | `string` | Name of the theme to use for the lightdm-slick-greeter. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.slick.theme.package` | `package` | The gnome-themes-extra package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.tiny.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable lightdm-tiny-greeter as the lightdm greeter. Note that this greeter starts only the default X session. You can configure the default X session using [](#opt-services.displayManager.defaultSession). |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.tiny.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Section to describe style and ui. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.tiny.label.pass` | `string` | The string to represent the pass_text label. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.lightdm.greeters.tiny.label.user` | `string` | The string to represent the user_text label. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.session` | `list of (attribute set)` | List of sessions supported with the command used to start each session. Each session script can set the {var}`waitPID` shell variable to make this script wait until the end of the user session. Each script is used to define either a window manager or a desktop manager. These can be differentiated by setting the attribute {var}`manage` either to `"window"` or `"desktop"`. The list of desktop manager and window manager should appear inside the display manager with the desktop manager name followed by the window manager name. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.sessionCommands` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Shell commands executed just before the window or desktop manager is started. These commands are not currently sourced for Wayland sessions. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.setupCommands` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Shell commands executed just after the X server has started. This option is only effective for display managers for which this feature is supported; currently these are LightDM, GDM and SDDM. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.startx.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the dummy "startx" pseudo-display manager, which allows users to start X manually via the `startx` command from a virtual terminal. ::: {.note} The X server will run under the current user, not as root. ::: |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.startx.extraCommands` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Shell commands to be added to the system-wide xinitrc script. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.startx.generateScript` | `boolean` | Whether to generate the system-wide xinitrc script (/etc/X11/xinit/xinitrc). This script will take care of setting up the session for systemd user services, running the window manager and cleaning up on exit. ::: {.note} This script will only be used by `startx` when both `.xinitrc` does not exists and the `XINITRC` environment variable is unset. ::: |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.sx.addAsSession` | `boolean` | Whether to add sx as a display manager session. Keep in mind that sx expects to be run from a TTY, so it may not work in your display manager. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.sx.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the "sx" pseudo-display manager, which allows users to start manually via the "sx" command from a vt shell. The X server runs under the user's id, not as root. The user must provide a ~/.config/sx/sxrc file containing session startup commands, see {manpage}`sx(1)`. This is not automatically generated from the desktopManager and windowManager settings. sx doesn't have a way to directly set X server flags, but it can be done by overriding its xorg-server dependency. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.sx.package` | `package` | The sx package to use. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.xpra.auth` | `string` | Authentication to use when connecting to xpra |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.xpra.bindTcp` | `null or string` | Bind xpra to TCP |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.xpra.desktop` | `null or string` | Start a desktop environment instead of seamless mode |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.xpra.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable xpra as display manager. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.xpra.extraOptions` | `list of string` | Extra xpra options |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.xpra.pulseaudio` | `boolean` | Whether to enable pulseaudio audio streaming. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.xserverArgs` | `list of string` | List of arguments for the X server. |
| `services.xserver.displayManager.xserverBin` | `absolute path` | Path to the X server used by display managers. |
| `services.xserver.dpi` | `null or signed integer` | Force global DPI resolution to use for X server. It's recommended to use this only when DPI is detected incorrectly; also consider using `Monitor` section in configuration file instead. |
| `services.xserver.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the X server. |
| `services.xserver.enableCtrlAltBackspace` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the DontZap option, which binds Ctrl+Alt+Backspace to forcefully kill X. This can lead to data loss and is disabled by default. |
| `services.xserver.enableTCP` | `boolean` | Whether to allow the X server to accept TCP connections. |
| `services.xserver.enableTearFree` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the TearFree option in the first Device section. |
| `services.xserver.excludePackages` | `list of package` | Which X11 packages to exclude from the default environment |
| `services.xserver.exportConfiguration` | `boolean` | Whether to symlink the X server configuration under {file}`/etc/X11/xorg.conf`. |
| `services.xserver.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Additional contents (sections) included in the X server configuration file |
| `services.xserver.extraDisplaySettings` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Lines to be added to every Display subsection of the Screen section. |
| `services.xserver.filesSection` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Contents of the first `Files` section of the X server configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.fontPath` | `null or string` | Set the X server FontPath. Defaults to null, which means the compiled in defaults will be used. See man xorg.conf for details. |
| `services.xserver.imwheel.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable IMWheel service. |
| `services.xserver.imwheel.extraOptions` | `list of string` | Additional command-line arguments to pass to {command}`imwheel`. |
| `services.xserver.imwheel.rules` | `attribute set of string` | Window class translation rules. /etc/X11/imwheelrc is generated based on this config which means this config is global for all users. See [official man pages](https://imwheel.sourceforge.net/imwheel.1.html) for more information. |
| `services.xserver.inputClassSections` | `list of strings concatenated with "\n"` | Content of additional InputClass sections of the X server configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.logFile` | `null or string` | Controls the file Xorg logs to. The default of `/dev/null` is set so that systemd services (like `displayManagers`) only log to the journal and don't create their own log files. Setting this to `null` will not pass the `-logfile` argument to Xorg which allows it to log to its default logfile locations instead (see `man Xorg`). You probably only want this behaviour when running Xorg manually (e.g. via `startx`). |
| `services.xserver.moduleSection` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Contents of the Module section of the X server configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.modules` | `list of absolute path` | Packages to be added to the module search path of the X server. |
| `services.xserver.monitorSection` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Contents of the first Monitor section of the X server configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.resolutions` | `list of (attribute set)` | The screen resolutions for the X server. The first element is the default resolution. If this list is empty, the X server will automatically configure the resolution. |
| `services.xserver.screenSection` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Contents of the first Screen section of the X server configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.serverFlagsSection` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Contents of the ServerFlags section of the X server configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.serverLayoutSection` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Contents of the ServerLayout section of the X server configuration file. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.accelFactor` | `null or string` | Cursor acceleration (how fast speed increases from minSpeed to maxSpeed). |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.additionalOptions` | `string` | Additional options for synaptics touchpad driver. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.buttonsMap` | `list of signed integer` | Remap touchpad buttons. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.dev` | `null or string` | Path for touchpad device. Set to null to apply to any auto-detected touchpad. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable touchpad support. Deprecated: Consider services.libinput.enable. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.fingersMap` | `list of signed integer` | Remap several-fingers taps. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.horizEdgeScroll` | `boolean` | Whether to enable horizontal edge drag-scrolling. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.horizTwoFingerScroll` | `boolean` | Whether to enable horizontal two-finger drag-scrolling. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.horizontalScroll` | `boolean` | Whether to enable horizontal scrolling (on touchpad) |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.maxSpeed` | `null or string` | Cursor speed factor for highest-speed finger motion. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.minSpeed` | `null or string` | Cursor speed factor for precision finger motion. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.palmDetect` | `boolean` | Whether to enable palm detection (hardware support required) |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.palmMinWidth` | `null or signed integer` | Minimum finger width at which touch is considered a palm |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.palmMinZ` | `null or signed integer` | Minimum finger pressure at which touch is considered a palm |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.scrollDelta` | `null or signed integer` | Move distance of the finger for a scroll event. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.tapButtons` | `boolean` | Whether to enable tap buttons. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.twoFingerScroll` | `boolean` | Whether to enable two-finger drag-scrolling. Overridden by horizTwoFingerScroll and vertTwoFingerScroll. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.vertEdgeScroll` | `boolean` | Whether to enable vertical edge drag-scrolling. |
| `services.xserver.synaptics.vertTwoFingerScroll` | `boolean` | Whether to enable vertical two-finger drag-scrolling. |
| `services.xserver.terminateOnReset` | `boolean` | Whether to terminate X upon server reset. |
| `services.xserver.updateDbusEnvironment` | `boolean` | Whether to update the DBus activation environment after launching the desktop manager. |
| `services.xserver.upscaleDefaultCursor` | `boolean` | Upscale the default X cursor to be more visible on high-density displays. Requires `config.services.xserver.dpi` to be set. |
| `services.xserver.verbose` | `null or signed integer` | Controls verbosity of X logging. |
| `services.xserver.videoDriver` | `null or string` | The name of the video driver for your graphics card. This option is obsolete; please set the {option}`services.xserver.videoDrivers` instead. |
| `services.xserver.videoDrivers` | `list of string` | The names of the video drivers the configuration supports. They will be tried in order until one that supports your card is found. Don't combine those with "incompatible" OpenGL implementations, e.g. free ones (mesa-based) with proprietary ones. For unfree "nvidia\*", the supported GPU lists are on https://www.nvidia.com/object/unix.html |
| `services.xserver.virtualScreen` | `null or (attribute set)` | Virtual screen size for Xrandr. |
| `services.xserver.wacom.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the Wacom touchscreen/digitizer/tablet. If you ever have any issues such as, try switching to terminal (ctrl-alt-F1) and back which will make Xorg reconfigure the device ? If you're not satisfied by the default behaviour you can override {option}`environment.etc."X11/xorg.conf.d/70-wacom.conf"` in configuration.nix easily. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager."2bwm".enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable 2bwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.afterstep.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable afterstep. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.awesome.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Awesome window manager. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.awesome.luaModules` | `list of package` | List of lua packages available for being used in the Awesome configuration. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.awesome.noArgb` | `boolean` | Disable client transparency support, which can be greatly detrimental to performance in some setups |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.awesome.package` | `package` | The awesome package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.berry.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable berry. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.bspwm.configFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the bspwm configuration file. If null, $HOME/.config/bspwm/bspwmrc will be used. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.bspwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable bspwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.bspwm.package` | `package` | The bspwm package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.bspwm.sxhkd.configFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the sxhkd configuration file. If null, $HOME/.config/sxhkd/sxhkdrc will be used. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.bspwm.sxhkd.package` | `package` | The sxhkd package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.clfswm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable clfswm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.clfswm.package` | `package` | The clfswm package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.cwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable cwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.dk.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable dk. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.dk.package` | `package` | The dk package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.dwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable dwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.dwm.extraSessionCommands` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Shell commands executed just before dwm is started. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.dwm.package` | `package` | The dwm package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.e16.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable e16. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.evilwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable evilwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.exwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable exwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.exwm.extraPackages` | `function that evaluates to a(n) list of package` | Extra packages available to Emacs. The value must be a function which receives the attrset defined in {var}`emacs.pkgs` as the sole argument. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.exwm.loadScript` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Emacs lisp code to be run after loading the user's init file. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.exwm.package` | `package` | The Emacs package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.fluxbox.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable fluxbox. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.fvwm2.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Fvwm2 window manager. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.fvwm2.gestures` | `boolean` | Whether or not to enable libstroke for gesture support |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.fvwm3.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Fvwm3 window manager. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.hackedbox.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable hackedbox. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.herbstluftwm.configFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the herbstluftwm configuration file. If left at the default value, $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/herbstluftwm/autostart will be used. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.herbstluftwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable herbstluftwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.herbstluftwm.package` | `package` | The herbstluftwm package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.hypr.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable hypr. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.i3.configFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the i3 configuration file. If left at the default value, $HOME/.i3/config will be used. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.i3.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable i3 window manager. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.i3.extraPackages` | `list of package` | Extra packages to be installed system wide. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.i3.extraSessionCommands` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Shell commands executed just before i3 is started. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.i3.package` | `package` | The i3 package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.i3.updateSessionEnvironment` | `boolean` | Whether to run dbus-update-activation-environment and systemctl import-environment before session start. Required for xdg portals to function properly. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.icewm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable icewm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.jwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable jwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.katriawm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable katriawm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.katriawm.package` | `package` | The katriawm package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.leftwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable leftwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.lwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable lwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.metacity.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable metacity. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.mlvwm.configFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the mlvwm configuration file. If left at the default value, $HOME/.mlvwmrc will be used. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.mlvwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Macintosh-like Virtual Window Manager. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.mwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable mwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.nimdow.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable nimdow. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.nimdow.package` | `package` | The nimdow package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.notion.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable notion. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.openbox.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable openbox. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.oxwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable oxwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.oxwm.package` | `package` | The oxwm package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.pekwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable pekwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.qtile.configFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to the qtile configuration file. If null, $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/qtile/config.py will be used. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.qtile.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable qtile. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.qtile.extraPackages` | `function that evaluates to a(n) list of package` | Extra Python packages available to Qtile. An example would be to include `python3Packages.qtile-extras` for additional unofficial widgets. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.qtile.package` | `package` | The qtile package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.ratpoison.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable ratpoison. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.sawfish.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable sawfish. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.smallwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable smallwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.spectrwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable spectrwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.stumpwm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable stumpwm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.stumpwm.package` | `package` | The stumpwm package to use. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.tinywm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable tinywm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.twm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable twm. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.windowlab.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable windowlab. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.windowmaker.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable windowmaker. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.wmderland.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable wmderland. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.wmderland.extraPackages` | `list of package` | Extra packages to be installed system wide. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.wmderland.extraSessionCommands` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Shell commands executed just before wmderland is started. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.wmii.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable wmii. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.xmonad.config` | `null or absolute path or string` | Configuration from which XMonad gets compiled. If no value is specified, a vanilla xmonad binary is put in PATH, which will attempt to recompile and exec your xmonad config from $HOME/.xmonad. This setup is then analogous to other (non-NixOS) linux distributions. If you do set this option, you likely want to use "launch" as your entry point for xmonad (as in the example), to avoid xmonad's recompilation logic on startup. Doing so will render the default "mod+q" restart key binding dysfunctional though, because that attempts to call your binary with the "--restart" command line option, unless you implement that yourself. You way mant to bind "mod+q" to `(restart "xmonad" True)` instead, which will just restart xmonad from PATH. This allows e.g. switching to the new xmonad binary after rebuilding your system with nixos-rebuild. For the same reason, ghc is not added to the environment when this option is set, unless {option}`enableConfiguredRecompile` is set to `true`. If you actually want to run xmonad with a config specified here, but also be able to recompile and restart it from a copy of that source in $HOME/.xmonad on the fly, set {option}`enableConfiguredRecompile` to `true` and implement something like "compileRestart" from the example. This should allow you to switch at will between the local xmonad and the one NixOS puts in your PATH. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.xmonad.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable xmonad. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.xmonad.enableConfiguredRecompile` | `boolean` | Enable recompilation even if {option}`config` is set to a non-null value. This adds the necessary Haskell dependencies (GHC with packages) to the xmonad binary's environment. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.xmonad.enableContribAndExtras` | `boolean` | Enable xmonad-{contrib,extras} in Xmonad. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.xmonad.extraPackages` | `function that evaluates to a(n) list of package` | Extra packages available to ghc when rebuilding Xmonad. The value must be a function which receives the attrset defined in {var}`haskellPackages` as the sole argument. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.xmonad.ghcArgs` | `list of string` | Command line arguments passed to the compiler (ghc) invocation when xmonad.config is set. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.xmonad.haskellPackages` | `attribute set` | haskellPackages used to build Xmonad and other packages. This can be used to change the GHC version used to build Xmonad and the packages listed in {var}`extraPackages`. |
| `services.xserver.windowManager.xmonad.xmonadCliArgs` | `list of string` | Command line arguments passed to the xmonad binary. |
| `services.xserver.xautolock.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable xautolock. |
| `services.xserver.xautolock.enableNotifier` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the notifier feature of xautolock. This publishes a notification before the autolock. |
| `services.xserver.xautolock.extraOptions` | `list of string` | Additional command-line arguments to pass to {command}`xautolock`. |
| `services.xserver.xautolock.killer` | `null or string` | The script to use when nothing has happened for as long as {option}`killtime` |
| `services.xserver.xautolock.killtime` | `signed integer` | Minutes xautolock waits until it executes the script specified in {option}`killer` (Has to be at least 10 minutes) |
| `services.xserver.xautolock.locker` | `string` | The script to use when automatically locking the computer. |
| `services.xserver.xautolock.notifier` | `null or string` | Notification script to be used to warn about the pending autolock. |
| `services.xserver.xautolock.notify` | `signed integer` | Time (in seconds) before the actual lock when the notification about the pending lock should be published. |
| `services.xserver.xautolock.nowlocker` | `null or string` | The script to use when manually locking the computer with {command}`xautolock -locknow`. |
| `services.xserver.xautolock.time` | `signed integer` | Idle time (in minutes) to wait until xautolock locks the computer. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.dir` | `absolute path` | Path used for -xkbdir xserver parameter. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.extraLayouts` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Extra custom layouts that will be included in the xkb configuration. Information on how to create a new layout can be found here: <https://www.x.org/releases/current/doc/xorg-docs/input/XKB-Enhancing.html#Defining_New_Layouts>. For more examples see <https://wiki.archlinux.org/index.php/X_KeyBoard_extension#Basic_examples> |
| `services.xserver.xkb.extraLayouts.<name>.compatFile` | `null or absolute path` | The path to the xkb compat file. This file sets the compatibility state, used to preserve compatibility with xkb-unaware programs. It must contain a `xkb_compat "name" { ... }` block. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.extraLayouts.<name>.description` | `string` | A short description of the layout. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.extraLayouts.<name>.geometryFile` | `null or absolute path` | The path to the xkb geometry file. This (completely optional) file describes the physical layout of keyboard, which maybe be used by programs to depict it. It must contain a `xkb_geometry "name" { ... }` block. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.extraLayouts.<name>.keycodesFile` | `null or absolute path` | The path to the xkb keycodes file. This file specifies the range and the interpretation of the raw keycodes sent by the keyboard. It must contain a `xkb_keycodes "name" { ... }` block. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.extraLayouts.<name>.languages` | `list of string` | A list of languages provided by the layout. (Use ISO 639-2 codes, for example: "eng" for english) |
| `services.xserver.xkb.extraLayouts.<name>.symbolsFile` | `null or absolute path` | The path to the xkb symbols file. This is the most important file: it defines which symbol or action maps to each key and must contain a `xkb_symbols "name" { ... }` block. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.extraLayouts.<name>.typesFile` | `null or absolute path` | The path to the xkb types file. This file specifies the key types that can be associated with the various keyboard keys. It must contain a `xkb_types "name" { ... }` block. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.layout` | `string` | X keyboard layout, or multiple keyboard layouts separated by commas. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.model` | `string` | X keyboard model. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.options` | `strings concatenated with ","` | X keyboard options; layout switching goes here. |
| `services.xserver.xkb.variant` | `string` | X keyboard variant. |
| `services.xserver.xrandrHeads` | `list of ((submodule) or string convertible to it)` | Multiple monitor configuration, just specify a list of XRandR outputs. The individual elements should be either simple strings or an attribute set of output options. If the element is a string, it is denoting the physical output for a monitor, if it's an attribute set, you must at least provide the {option}`output` option. The monitors will be mapped from left to right in the order of the list. By default, the first monitor will be set as the primary monitor if none of the elements contain an option that has set {option}`primary` to `true`. ::: {.note} Only one monitor is allowed to be primary. ::: Be careful using this option with multiple graphic adapters or with drivers that have poor support for XRandR, unexpected things might happen with those. |
| `services.xserver.xrandrHeads.*.monitorConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra lines to append to the `Monitor` section verbatim. Available options are documented in the MONITOR section in {manpage}`xorg.conf(5)`. |
| `services.xserver.xrandrHeads.*.output` | `string` | The output name of the monitor, as shown by {manpage}`xrandr(1)` invoked without arguments. |
| `services.xserver.xrandrHeads.*.primary` | `boolean` | Whether this head is treated as the primary monitor, |
